action.hypothesis: With the `gated` mainline stalled for nine rounds, the highest-value Round 62 matched architecture probe is `group_topk`: it preserves the same static decoder-library weighted-sum export contract and fixed `K=128`, while changing only support formation to a group-local winner scheme that could reduce selection complexity if reconstruction stays competitive. This probe answers one question only: is `group_topk` intrinsically strong enough under the matched `signum`/`2.0e-3`/`EF=12` recipe to merit any later follow-up.
action.summary: Run one matched architecture probe by switching `ARCHITECTURE` from `gated` to `group_topk` and keeping `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` fixed.
action.self_review: This respects the Round 62 policy: one probe only, one main variable only, and the full mainline recipe stays matched except for architecture. `group_topk` is a better probe than heavier multi-branch variants because it is directly LUTurbo-compatible and has a plausible path to lower online selection cost rather than only improving training-side expressiveness.
notes_to_memory: Round 62 uses the single allowed matched architecture probe on `group_topk` rather than expanding the search broadly.
`group_topk` remains directly compatible with LUTurbo because reconstruction is still a finite weighted sum over a static decoder library; only the encoder-side selection rule changes.

The main deployment-relevant reason to test `group_topk` is not just FVU but whether grouped winner selection looks promising enough to justify later work on lower selection overhead under the same decoder contract.
next_hypotheses: If `group_topk` is clearly worse than matched `gated` at `K=128`, discard the family and return to the `gated` mainline next round.
If `group_topk` is roughly competitive, record it as a potentially deployment-friendlier selector but still return to the `gated` mainline next round per policy.
If `group_topk` unexpectedly beats matched `gated`, schedule at most one later follow-up after the mandated return to the `gated` mainline.
